title: teucrium leucocladum var. leucocladum
intro: Teucrium leucocladum var. leucocladum, also known as White Leadwort, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae, native to the Balkan Peninsula. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of White Leadwort include its ability to soothe respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, White Leadwort is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although it should be used with caution and under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to its potential toxicity.
